title: /kernel
summary_text: Short answer: yes—`/kernel` requires a `BLOCKED` state when it cannot establish either the normal or degraded-allowed three-voice shape. The rule is explicit in the local prompt: valid shapes are `codex + glm + specialist` (normal) and `codex + specialist + specialist` (degraded-allowed), and `BLOCKED` is valid only when neither can be established. See [`/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:62`]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L62) and [`/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:65`]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md#L65). The `/k` alias repeats the same contract in [`/.codex/prompts/k.md:64`]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/k.md#L64). Static contract checks enforce this text directly: `scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` asserts presence of both “valid three-voice shape cannot be established” and the `BLOCKED`-only-when-both-shapes-fail language in `kernel.md` lines 100–101 of the checker. See [`/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:100`]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L100) and [`/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:101`]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L101). Bootstrap validation also fails closed if required shape elements are missing: normal requires `glm` and at least one specialist plus credentials; degraded-allowed requires two specialists. That is enforced in `scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh` (`launch_validate_shape`, lines 481–487). See [`/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:481`]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh#L481). Runtime state mapping then emits `BLOCKED` for any non-healthy/non-degraded shape via `shape_from_health_state` mapping. See [`/scripts/lib/kernel-4pane-surface.sh:91`]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-4pane-surface.sh#L91).
